title: Impact by the collision of the White Moon
display_title: Impact by the collision of the White Moon
wikitext:
[]: Impact by the collision of the White Moon?             Quote Post by Slepton » Thu Jul 02, 2015 2:10 am So basically what we know is that the collision of the Black Moon causes the first Impact (probably from the Dead Sea Scrolls). Although I cannot point in which page it was, in Wiki I have read that the Black Moon and the White Moon are physically quite similar, so it is a mystery for me that why there was not any impact regarding the collision of the White Moon. I think, if this reasoning is accepted, there is another re-reading of this situation as in the following: Rather than the collision directly causing the first Impact; it is the cause of inhabitants of the moons ending up on the same planet contrary to the First Ancestral Race's intentions. If so we would conclude that the causation relation between the collision and the First Impact is not a direct one, but an indirect one. Of course I think somewhere I am sadly mistaken so I would really appreciate it if someone could clarify it since I am failing to see it.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Jul 02, 2015 3:33 am I understood it as the White Moon arriving early in the planet's formation and therefore not causing any impact since there was no life around on Earth at the time, but the Black Moon, coming later and after the White Moon had begun its work, caused an impact when the "reset button" for life was hit. Adam was impaled by her own Spear of Longinus and life from Lilith began to spread.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 02, 2015 3:47 am Pretty sure First Impact is just supposed to be a fictionalized version of Giant Impact, with the Black Moon acting as the impactor. http://wiki.evageeks.org/Classified_Information_(Translation)#The_Second_Angel http://wiki.evageeks.org/Classified_Information_(Translation)#Geofront http://wiki.evageeks.org/File:07_C080_textbook-overlay.jpg Oddly, the neutrino scan of the White Moon in 21' says "possibly created at time of Giant Impact". I have no idea how that's supposed to make any sense at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Jul 02, 2015 9:17 am Adam's White Moon had intended to arrive on Earth and successfully landed; Lilith's Black Moon arrived on Earth by accident and crashed into its surface, causing First Impact.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jul 02, 2015 9:31 am Sachi wrote: Adam's White Moon had intended to arrive on Earth and successfully landed; Lilith's Black Moon arrived on Earth by accident and crashed into its surface, causing First Impact.

[]:             Quote Post by Logoguy » Sat Jul 04, 2015 3:05 am Adam and her progeny are considered the true successors of Earth. While Lilith and her progeny are considered usurpers; Seele obviously prefer the former over the latter. They also hypocritically denounce the creation of gods, while they themselves were secretly aspiring to achieve the same status. (evidently they didn't think much of humans). So to break it down: First Impact was caused by the arrival of the Black Moon, which rendered Adam based lifeforms (Angels) inert for 4 billion years.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Sat Jul 04, 2015 1:08 pm I wish Anno had made the White Moon Earth's actual Moon, just so someone could say "That's no Moon".

[]:             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Sat Jul 04, 2015 1:12 pm Given its state in Q, your wish may actually get fulfilled

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Sat Jul 04, 2015 2:41 pm ^ Yeah... but it's not a surprise
